question: Adjective of Body
explanation: All the words are adjective of Body: Corporal, Bodily, Corporeal.

question: As unstable as ............
explanation: As unstable as water. (Simile/idiom)

question: Give one-word substitution to the following: Life history of a man written by himself is called
explanation: Autobiography is a life history written by oneself.

question: Change the following into passive voice: Rita writes a letter to him everyday.

options.A: To him a letter was written by Rita everyday
options.B: A letter was written to him by Rita everyday
options.C: A letter is written to him by Rita everyday
options.D: He is being written a letter by Rita everyday

explanation: A letter is written to him by Rita everyday. (Simple Present Passive: Subject + is/am/are + V3)

question: Change the following verb into Noun: Approve
explanation: After changing the verb Approve into Noun, it will be Approval.

question: Choose the correct figure of speech in the following sentence: 'Fair is foul and foul is fair.'
explanation: 'Fair is foul and foul is fair'; the correct figure of speech is Oxymoron. Oxymoron is a figure of speech in which apparently contradictory terms appear in conjunction.

question: Which of the following was a 'Nature Poet'?
explanation: William Wordsworth was a nature poet and a major English Romantic poet, born on April 7, 1770 at Cockermouth, England.

question: Name the famous poet who wrote the following lines: "Where the mind is without fear and the head is held high...."
explanation: The above line is written by Rabindranath Tagore.

question: 'The Castaway' is a famous short story written by which of the following author?
explanation: 'The Castaway' is a famous short story written by Rabindranath Tagore. It depicts the emotional journey of a young orphan Nilkanta.

question: Choose the Synonym for the following word: Derogatory
explanation: Synonym of the word Derogatory is Humiliating. Derogatory means showing a critical or disrespectful attitude.

question: 'Poos Ki Raat' is written by which famous author?
explanation: 'Poos Ki Raat' is written by Munshi Premchand. It is a story of a tenant farmer named Halku and his plight against land lordship prevalent.

question: Select the word which has the same meaning and can be used in the same context as the underlined part: I. The stadium can SEAT more than two hundred spectators. II. Mohan is willing to ADJUST his tour programme.
explanation: The word which has the same meaning is Accommodate.

question: Point out the Figure of Speech used in: 'Integrity is the backbone of character.'
explanation: 'Integrity is the backbone of character', the figure of speech here is Metaphor. Metaphor is implied Simile.

question: Find out the grammatically correct sentence

options.A: This is the road to go
options.B: Let us aim to do good
options.C: Take down his address in your copy

explanation: The grammatically correct sentence is 'I wrote to him yesterday'.

question: The poem 'On his Blindness' is composed by which of the following poets?
explanation: 'On his Blindness' is one of the best known sonnets of John Milton. It was first published in Milton's 1673 poems.

question: Pick out the incorrectly spelt word
explanation: Exeggarate is incorrectly spelt; it should be Exaggerate. Exaggerate means to represent as being larger, better or worse than it actually is.

question: Complete the sentence with appropriate word: Your hand writing should be
explanation: Your hand writing should be intelligible (= easy to read/understand).

question: Give the proper relative Pronoun: All.....glitters is not gold.
explanation: All that glitters is not gold.

question: Find out the appropriate meaning of the given word: Etymology

options.D: A study of the origin of words

explanation: Etymology is a kind of study which deals with the origin of words.

question: Find out the appropriate meaning of the word: Inimitable
explanation: The appropriate meaning of Inimitable is Incapable of being imitated.

question: Choose one word for the following expression: 'A lover of mankind'
explanation: Philanthropist – A lover of mankind.

question: Find out the incorrect sentence:

options.A: The apples are grown in many different countries.
options.B: Books are essential to a student.
options.C: Chess is a game which requires great patience.
options.D: The Sharmas live at Elgin Road.

explanation: The incorrect sentence is 'The apples are grown in many different countries.' The definite article 'The' is used wrongly before apples as it refers to all apples in general categories, not a particular variety.

question: Use the correct form of Verb: He......... in Allahabad for five years.
explanation: He has been living in Allahabad for five years. (Present Perfect Continuous Tense)

question: Choose the correct meaning of the following phrase: Beck and call
explanation: The correct meaning of the phrase 'Beck and call' is under absolute control.

question: Choose the correct Antonyms for the following word: Expedite
explanation: Expedite means to hasten, so Antonym is Delay.

question: Fill in the blank with the correct Preposition: The Judge acquitted him....... the murder charge.
explanation: The Judge acquitted him of the murder charge.

question: Fill in the blank with the Past Perfect Continuous Tense: The doctor was very tired as he ......... alone.
explanation: The doctor was very tired as he had been working alone. (Past Perfect Continuous Tense)

question: Find out the correct word of the grammatical category of the given word: 'Adjective of Accent'
explanation: It is 'Accentual'.

question: Fill in the blanks. Ritu is ........ most active girl in ........ family.
explanation: Here reference is being made to a specific girl and her family. So, 'the' is used in both the places. Hence sentence will be 'Ritu is most active girl in the family'.

question: A person who regards the whole world as his country is called
explanation: Cosmopolitan is a person who is free from local or national prejudices and feels a sense of belongingness wherever he might go.

question: 'Quarried' means
explanation: 'Quarry' means to extract materials such as stone, slate, etc, from an open pit.

question: Change the following into past perfect continuous tense. The boys are playing cricket.
explanation: Past Perfect Continuous tense has the following form: subject + had + been + verb (continuous form). So 'had been playing' is the correct option. Hence sentence will be 'The boys had been playing cricket'.

question: The lesson 'Torch Bearers' is written by which of the following writers?
explanation: The lesson 'Torch Bearers' is written by WM Ryburn.

question: Choose the correct figure of speech in the following sentence. 'The wind lies asleep in the arms of dawn.'
explanation: Personification is a figure of speech where human qualities are given to animals, objects or ideas. Here wind and dawn (in animate object) are being thought of as living beings, i.e. they are being personified humans.

question: Choose the correct meaning of the following phrase. Foot the bill
explanation: 'Foot the bill' is an idiom which means 'to pay for something'.

question: Indicate the tense of the following sentence. I have sent a letter to him.
explanation: I have sent a letter to him is in present perfect tense.

question: Phonetic is the Science of
explanation: Phonetic is the study of the sounds made by human voice in speech.

question: Language learning starts from
explanation: Listening is the first stage where in a person start learning the nuances of a language.

question: General aim of prose teaching is to help the students to develop
explanation: General aim of prose teaching is to help the students to develop interest in reading, critical thinking and comprehension ability.

question: Which Indian reformist supported English education?
explanation: Raja Ram Mohan Roy (22 May 1772–27 September 1833) was the founder of the Brahmo Sabha movement in 1828, who engendered the Brahmo Samaj. He translated Vedic scriptures into English and was a supporter of English education.

question: Choose the correct meaning of the following phrase. To bring to light
explanation: 'To bring to light' means to present or reveal something.

question: Choose the synonym for the following word. Consequence
explanation: Consequence means the result or outcome of something.

question: Change the following verb into noun. Succeed
explanation: Successful is an adjective. Succeeding is a verb form. Successfully is an adverb. Success is the noun form of Succeed.

question: 'Language is one of the most important and characteristics forms of human behaviour'. It is the statement of
explanation: 'Language is one of the most important and characteristics forms of human behaviour'. It is the statement given by Gleason.

question: Fill in the blank with correct preposition. He is blind ......... his own fault.
explanation: 'to' is the correct answer. He is blind to his own fault.

question: The poem 'The Nation Builders' is composed by which of the following poets?
explanation: 'The Nation Builders' is a short poem composed by Ralf Waldo Emerson.

question: Choose the correct word from the words given below to complete the sentence. The soldiers ............. through the town.
explanation: The soldiers marched through the town.

question: Which of the following is in possessive case?
explanation: A possessive case shows ownership. My, his, here, yours, mine, whose – these all show ownership. 'Mine' is in possessive case.

question: What is difficult to do without paying large amount of money?

options.B: To hire an auto for a short distance

explanation: Boarding a flight is not a cheap affair.

question: Name the famous poet who wrote the following line. 'They also serve who only stand and wait'.
explanation: This is a quote from the English poet John Milton's poem 'On His Blindness'.

question: Complete the following sentence by using the correct alternative. Walk carefully lest
explanation: 'lest' is followed by 'should'. Hence: Walk carefully lest you should fall down.

question: Find out the incorrect sentence.

options.A: The quality of the mangoes was not good
options.B: You, he and I am good friends
options.C: The 'Arabian Nights' is still a great favourite
options.D: Each of the scholars has done well

explanation: Use 'are' instead of 'am'. The correct sentence is: You, he and I are good friends.

question: Change the following into passive voice. 'Do not insult your elders'.

options.A: Let not your elders be insulted
options.B: Your elders let not be insulted
options.C: Let your elders not be insulted by you

explanation: After changing into passive voice 'Let your elders not be insulted by you'.

question: Find out the grammatically correct sentence.

options.A: Politics are the subject which is taught at degree level
options.B: It is you who are responsible for the downfall
options.C: I, you and he is good friend
options.D: Ganga is a famous river of India

explanation: In the first sentence, 'is' should be used after 'politics'. In the third sentence, 'are' should be used instead of 'is' and in the fourth sentence, use 'The Ganga' instead of 'Ganga'.

question: State the part of speech of the underlined word. He looked above in the sky.
explanation: 'above' modifies the verb 'Looked' that appears before it. So, it is used here as an adverb.

question: Choose the correct option. The passengers alighted ......... the bus.
explanation: The passengers alighted from the bus.

question: Find where the error is in the sentence. I am reaching (a) / school (b) / in time (c) / everyday. (d)
explanation: It should be 'I reach school in time everyday'.

question: Point out figure of speech in the following sentence. 'And having nothing he hath all'.
explanation: Oxymoron has been used in above sentence. An oxymoron is a figure of speech in which seemingly contradictory terms appear side by side.

question: The substitution table method was adopted by
explanation: The substitution table method was adopted by HE Palmer.

question: Choose the correct word to fill in the blank. Ten miles......... a long way to walk.
explanation: Unit always follows singular number. In the present sentence 10 miles is a unit. Hence option (b) is correct. Correct sentence: Ten miles is a long way to walk.

question: Choose the appropriate synonym of the word written in Italics. There can be different ways of solving the same problem.
explanation: The synonym of the word ways is methods.

question: Write one word for the following group of words. Which cannot be read?
explanation: Which cannot be read is illegible.

question: Choose the opposite of following word. Particularly
explanation: Opposite of Particularly is 'Generally'. Particularly means especially. Generally means considering the whole of someone or something.

question: Phonetic aspect of language deals with
explanation: Phonetic aspect of language deals with sound, spelling and pronunciation.

question: Causes of spelling errors are
explanation: Causes of spelling errors are: Silent letters; Poor pronunciation; one letter different sounds.

question: Identify the odd word from the following.
explanation: Sorrow is the odd one which means a feeling of great sadness.

question: Which of the following is an 'Oxymoron'?
explanation: Deafening silence is an Oxymoron. Deafening means extremely loud. Whereas silence means complete quiet. Oxymoron – two words used together that have, or seem to have, opposite meanings.

question: Give one word substitution for the following. One hundred years
explanation: One hundred years is a century.

question: Fill in the blanks with the correct articles. ........... Taj Mahal is ........... monument symbolizing love.
explanation: 'The' has been used before any historical place or monument, where monument is a countable noun which starts with consonant sound, hence 'a' is used here.

question: Fill in the blanks with the correct prepositions. I tried ......... relieve them ......... their poverty.
explanation: I tried to relieve them of their poverty.

question: Choose the correct phrase to complete the proverb. God helps those who..........
explanation: God help those who help themselves.

question: Fill in the blank with correct preposition. When she parted .............. her parents, her eyes were full of tears.
explanation: For separation we use preposition 'from'. Correct sentence: When she parted from her parents, her eyes were full of tears.

question: Fill in the blank with past perfect tense. The train ............. the platform before I reached the station.
explanation: With Past perfect tense, Had + a Past participle has been used.

question: Choose the synonym of the following word. inundate
explanation: The synonym of the word Inundate is to flood.

question: The girl child 'Lucy' is in poems written by
explanation: The girl child 'Lucy' appears in poems of William Wordsworth, one of the greatest nature poets.

question: Choose the incorrect part of the sentence. The man/standing next to/the pillar is/my older brother.
explanation: Elder is used only of person and is now confined to member of same family. Hence option (d) is incorrect. Correct sentence: The man standing next to the pillar is my elder brother.

question: Fill in the blank with the correct idiom. I ......... of Rama at the theater but she did not see me.
explanation: Caught a glimpse is the correct option.

question: Fill in the blank with the appropriate modal. The villagers .......... read and write their mother tongue.
explanation: Modal Auxiliary verb 'can' is used to show capacity or ability in present tense. Hence option (b) is correct. Correct sentence: The villager can read and write their mother tongue.

question: Choose the correct form of the verb to fill in the blank. We .......... the matter yesterday.
explanation: Discuss, enter, resemble, normally these verbs are followed by direct objects without preposition. Hence option (c) is correct.

question: Fill in the blank with the correct prepositional phrase. The deaf learn to communicate with one another .......... sign language.
explanation: By means of is the correct option.

question: Choose the best word to complete the sentence. He was tired and ill and .......... on the stairs and fell all the way down.
explanation: He was tired and ill and stumbled on the stairs and fell all the way down. Stumble means to step awkwardly while walking or running and fall or begin to fall.

question: Find out the part which has an error in the following sentence. They invited Jaya and I / to the function / to be held in the next month.

options.C: to be held in the next month

explanation: Pronouns are used in objective case when they are used as object of a sentence. Hence option (a) is incorrect. Pronoun I should be used as 'me'. Correct sentence: They invited Jaya and me to the function to be held in the next month.

question: Choose the alternative which best expresses the meaning of the phrase/idiom. The little girl with her flawless performance stole the show.
explanation: Stole the show means won everybody's praise.

question: Fill in the blank with the most suitable article. We were watching .......... news on BBC last evening.
explanation: 'the' is the best option.

question: Fill in the blank with the most suitable adverb from those provided. It took us ............. five hours to reach the airport.
explanation: It took us nearly five hours to reach the airport.

question: Choose the appropriate preposition. We have been living here ............ six months.
explanation: 'Since' is used for 'point of time' while 'for' is used for 'period of time'. In the present sentence six months is period of time. Hence option (d) is correct. Correct sentence: We have been living here for six months.

question: Which of the following 'laugh'?
explanation: Hyenas laugh.

question: Mark the synonym of the given word. Carcass
explanation: Synonym of the given word Carcass is Corpse. Corpse means dead body.

question: What kind of adjective is used in the following sentence? These mangoes are ripe.
explanation: Demonstrative Adjective is used in the sentence 'These mangoes are ripe'. Demonstrative Adjective: Demonstrative Adjectives are Adjectives that are used to modify a noun so that we know which specific person, place or thing is mentioned. Here in the present sentence 'These' modifies noun 'Mangoes' so 'These' is used as a demonstrative adjective.

question: Choose the correct word to complete the following sentence: He insisted ______ seeing the documents.
explanation: In the blank preposition 'on' is appropriate because verb 'Insist' always followed by preposition 'on'. Hence option (a) is correct. Correct sentence: He insisted on seeing the documents.

question: How did Luz Long help Josse Owens?

options.A: Luz Long advised Josse to draw a line a few inches behind the board and aim at making his take off from there.
options.B: Luz Long advised Josse to take rest for a few minutes.
options.C: Luz Long advised Josse to forget that he was participating in a competition.
options.D: Luz Long advised Josse not to accept the Master Race Theory of Hitler.

explanation: Correct option is (a): Luz Long advised Josse to draw a line a few inches behind the board and aim at making his take off from there.

question: Complete the following: Man is born, according to H.W. Longfellow.
explanation: Option (c) is correct. In the poem 'A Psalm of Life' composed by Henry Wordsworth Longfellow, the last stanza's first line is: 'Let us, then, be up and doing / with a heart for any fate', which makes clear that Man is born to be up and doing.

question: Complete the following sentence by choosing the correct option: No sooner did we go out on the road ______ it began to rain heavily.
explanation: 'No sooner ——than' is a structure. Such structures as 'hardly ——when/before' and 'scarcely ——when/before' are also used. Hence option (b) is correct. Correct sentence: No sooner did we go out on the road than it began to rain heavily.

question: The correct feminine of 'ram' is
explanation: Feminine of 'Ram' is 'Ewe'. Hence option (c) is correct.

question: Complete the following sentence with the correct option: ——— is the best story?
explanation: Correct sentence is – Yours is the best story.

question: Identify the correct reported form of the given sentence: Ravi said to his friend, 'work hard'.

options.A: Ravi said to his friend you must work hard.
options.B: Ravi asked his friend that he must work hard.
options.C: Ravi advised his friend to work hard.
options.D: Ravi ordered his friend to work hard.

explanation: Correct reported form is option (c): Ravi advised his friend to work hard. 'Work hard' is an imperative sentence. When we change these type of sentence into indirect speech: (I) We changed reporting verb say/says or said into order, request, urged, begged according to the sense of the sentence. (II) We used conjunction 'to'.

question: Choose the correct figures of speech in the following sentence: Her mother too upon this occasion felt a pleasing distress.
explanation: In the sentence – 'Her mother too upon this occasion felt a pleasing distress' the figure of speech is Oxymoron. Oxymoron: In this figure of speech two terms which are opposite in meaning are placed side by side and they form one lovely phrase. Here in the present sentence words 'Pleasing' and 'distress' are opposite.

question: Choose the word opposite in the meaning to the given word: FRAILTY
explanation: Correct opposite of the word 'Frailty' is 'strength' which means the quality or state of being physically strong.

question: Didactic poetry means:
explanation: Didactic poetry: Didactic poetry refers to poems that contain a clear, moral or message or purpose to convey to its reader, means poetry which teaches.

question: Fill in the blank with the suitable form of verb: Either Munni or Chunni ______ lost her pen.
explanation: When two subjects are joined by 'or' the verb is usually singular. Hence option (a) 'has' is correct. Either Munni or Chunni has lost her pen.

question: Point out the word that has been wrongly used in this sentence: If you lose your passport in a foreign country it will effect you badly.
explanation: 'effect' word is wrongly used in the present sentence. There should be 'affect' in place of 'effect'. Correct sentence: If you lose your passport in a foreign country it will affect you badly.

question: Poetry teaching should be basically a process of
explanation: Poetry teaching should be basically a process of joy.

question: What are the two productive skills?
explanation: Speaking and writing are the two productive skills. Productive skill: The productive skills are speaking and writing, because learners doing these need to produce language. They are also known as active skills.

question: Linguistics is a study of
explanation: Linguistic is a study of human language.

question: Dyslexia is associated mainly with difficulties in
explanation: Dyslexia: Dyslexia is a specific learning disability in reading.

question: The purpose of 'rapid reading' is
explanation: The purpose of 'rapid reading' is for specific detail.

question: What does the Ganges symbolize, according to Jawaharlal Nehru?

options.A: It is a symbol of India's prosperity.
options.B: It is a symbol of India's age-long culture and civilization.
options.C: It is a symbol of changing seasons of India.
options.D: It is a symbol of India's struggle for freedom.

explanation: According to Jawaharlal Nehru the Ganges symbolizes as a symbol of India's age-long culture and civilization. Source: High school English text book U.P. Board, Prose Chapter No. 3 Lesson – The Ganga written by Pt. Jawahar Lal Nehru.

question: What damaged the crop in Lencho's field?
explanation: Hailstones damaged the crop in Lencho's field. Source: High school English text book U.P. Board, Prose Chapter No. 2 'A letter to God' by G.L. Fuentes.

question: What does Lorenzo order when he learns that Portia is on her way to Belmont?

options.A: A banquet to welcome the lady of the house
options.C: A ring to match the one she once gave to Bassanio

explanation: Lorenzo orders music when he learns that Portia is on her way to Belmont. Source: The Merchant of Venice by William Shakespeare.

question: In court how does Antonio react to Shylock's insistence on collecting his pound of flesh?

options.B: He vows that he will meet Shylock's hatred with patience.
options.D: He makes an impassioned plea to the court to intervene on his behalf.

explanation: Antonio vows that he will meet Shylock's hatred with patience.

question: According to Lorenzo's plan, how will Jessica escape from her father's house?

options.A: She will disguise herself as Lorenzo's torch bearer and slip out undetected.
options.B: She will leave during the night while Shylock is asleep.
options.C: She will take her father to a large public auction and get lost in the crowd.
options.D: She will fake her own death.

explanation: Jessica escape from her father's house by disguising herself as Lorenzo's torch bearer and slipping out undetected.

question: How does Shylock initially describe his demand for a pound of flesh to Bassanio and Antonio?

options.B: As his way of being charitable
options.D: As a means of generating friendship

explanation: Shylock initially describes his demand for a pound of flesh to Bassanio and Antonio as an opportunity for revenge.

question: What reason does Antonio give for being sad in the opening scene of the play?

options.A: He stands to lose a fortune in his present business ventures.
options.B: He owes a fantastic sum of money to Shylock.
options.D: The woman he loves does not return his feelings.

explanation: Antonio gives no reason for being sad in the opening scene of the play.

question: Whom does Bassanio agree to bring with him to Belmont?
explanation: Bassanio agrees to bring Gratiano with him to Belmont.

question: Point out the error in the following incorrect sentence: I can learn poems more easy than speeches.
explanation: Option (c) is incorrect (more easy) there should be – easier. Correct sentence: I can learn poems easier than speeches.

question: Point out the sentence in the active voice.

options.A: Why don't you have your eyes tested?
options.C: Boys could not have done all this damage.
options.D: Steps had been cut in the ice by previous climbers.

explanation: Option (c) is in Active voice. Hence option (c) is correct.

question: Point out the correct word to bring out the meaning in the sentence: Women have always dressed to _____ figure flaws.
explanation: Option (d) is correct in the blank of the sentence. Camouflage means a way of hiding something by painting it or covering it with leaves or branches to make it harder to see. Correct sentence: Women have always dressed to camouflage figure flaws.

question: Point out the correct indirect speech of the sentence. "Would you like to come with us?" they said.

options.A: They asked me to go with them.
options.B: They asked me if I would like to come with them.
options.C: They invited me to go with them.
options.D: They told me to come with them.

explanation: Correct indirect speech is option (b). Rule: (i) If reported speech is in interrogative, we use conjunction 'if' or 'whether'. (ii) We change reporting verb say, says or said into ask, asks or asked. So correct indirect speech is: They asked me if I would like to come with them.

question: Which of the following is a correct expression?
explanation: Among all the given options, only option (a) 'As cheerful as a lark' is correct. It is an example of the Simile figure of speech which means 'extremely happy'.

question: The sentence, 'This is the house in which I was born', is
explanation: 'This is the house in which I was born' is a complex sentence. A complex sentence has at least one clause; in this sentence 'in which I was born' is an adjective clause.

question: Which of the following words is a preposition?
explanation: Among the four given words, option (a) 'Beyond' is a Preposition.

question: Which of the following words is an adverb?
explanation: Among the given four options, only option (a) 'Apace' is an adverb meaning 'at a fast pace'. Face – noun / verb, Meek – adjective (quiet, humble), Fury – noun (extreme anger).

question: Join the following sentences to make a simple sentence: The coffee isn't too strong. It won't keep us awake.

options.A: (a) The coffee isn't too strong to keep us awake.
options.B: (b) The coffee isn't too strong and will not keep us awake.
options.C: (c) The coffee isn't too strong so it won't keep us awake.
options.D: (d) The coffee isn't too strong so as to keep us awake.

explanation: A Simple Sentence has only one Finite verb. Only option (a) has been given in that form. Therefore, option (a) is correct.

question: Fill in the blank with suitable pronoun: My son and my daughter are very fond of ……
explanation: 'Each other' is used when there are two people. My son and my daughter are very fond of each other. 'Each other' is used when talking about two people.

question: Give one word for 'one who hates mankind'.
explanation: One who hates mankind is called 'Misanthrope'.

question: Transform the following sentence into a simple sentence ending with 'book': Let the book be given.
explanation: 'Let the book be given' is in Passive voice. The Active voice equivalent is 'Give the book'.

question: Transform the following sentence into a direct sentence: I asked Sheela if I might know her father.

options.A: (a) I said to Sheela, 'May I know your father?'
options.B: (b) I told Sheela, 'May I know your father?'
options.C: (c) I asked Sheela, 'Can I know your father?'
options.D: (d) I questioned Sheela, 'Will I know your father?'

explanation: To convert into Direct Speech: (i) 'asked' changes to 'said to'; (ii) conjunction 'if' is removed and inverted commas are used; (iii) 'might' changes to 'may' and the structure becomes Interrogative. Therefore option (a) is correct.

question: Fill in the blank to complete the following sentence: The Ministers are responsible ……. the Chief Minister of their State for their work.
explanation: Option (b) 'to' will be filled in the blank. 'Responsible to Somebody' means accountable to someone.

question: Complete the following sentence with correct conjunction: ……….he had not paid his bill, his electricity was cut off.
explanation: The conjunction 'As' will be used in the blank. As he had not paid his bill, his electricity was cut off. (Since he had not paid his bill, his electricity was cut off.)

question: Choose the correct figure of speech in the following sentence: Death lays his icy hand on kings.
explanation: In the sentence 'Death lays his icy hand on kings', Personification (Maanvikaran alankar) has been used. Death has been presented as a human being.

question: The study of population is known as
explanation: The study of population is known as Demography (Janasankhya). Ecology – Environmental science; Astronomy – Science of outer space; Genealogy – study of the history of a family's ancestors.

question: Give the meaning of the following proverb: 'A bird in hand is worth two in a bush'

options.A: (a) To catch two birds with your hands
options.B: (b) To be dissatisfied with what you have
options.C: (c) It is better to be satisfied with what you have than to try to get what is not yours

explanation: The meaning of the proverb 'A bird in hand is worth two in a bush' can be expressed by option (c): It is better to be satisfied with what you have than to try to get what is not yours.

question: Which of the following sounds is NOT associated with the sound of a bell?
explanation: Chime, Jingle and Ring are all associated with the sound of a bell. Therefore, option (d) Dang is NOT associated with the sound of a bell.

question: Fill in the blank with the correct preposition: Are you sorry……. what you have done?
explanation: Option (b) 'for' will fill the blank. 'Sorry for something' means ashamed/regretful about a thing.

question: Pick out the correct synonym of the following word: Attenuate
explanation: Attenuate means to make something lighter or weaker. 'Make thin' means to make thin/slim. Therefore, the synonym of 'Attenuate' is 'Make thin'. Repent means to regret.

question: Pick out the compound sentence.

options.A: (a) To add to their troubles the servant ran away.
options.B: (b) Having no money with me, I could not give the beggar anything.
options.C: (c) I loved him because he was my friend.
options.D: (d) The servant ran away and added to their troubles.

explanation: The servant ran away and added to their troubles is a compound sentence. Compound sentences are usually joined by: and, not only…but also, either…or, neither…nor, yet, so conjunctions. In option (c) 'because' makes the following sentence a dependent clause, so it is not a compound sentence.

question: Select the most appropriate preposition to complete the following sentence: Why are you always so suspicious………me?
explanation: The preposition 'of' will be used in the blank. 'Suspicious of Somebody/Something' means to be suspicious of someone or something.

question: Choose the correct alternative to complete the following sentence: The Headmaster……. to speak to you.
explanation: Option (a) 'wants' will fill the blank. The Headmaster wants to speak to you.

question: The word 'icy' in the passage is a/an
explanation: In the passage, 'icy' is an adjective that describes the continent (it is telling the quality of continent).

question: The word 'desert' in the passage is a
explanation: In the passage, 'desert' is used as a noun meaning a barren area.

question: The word 'windiest' in the passage is a
explanation: The word 'windiest' is the superlative form of the adjective 'windy'.

question: Has Antarctica always been fixed in the South Pole?
explanation: According to the passage, Antarctica has not always been at the South Pole; it shifted to the South Pole later (mentioned in line 16 of the passage).

question: Who are the earliest known inhabitants of Antarctica?

options.D: (d) It has always been a desert

explanation: Dinosaurs were the earliest known inhabitants of Antarctica.

question: The writer believes that half-educated persons are not able to

options.D: (d) appreciate hidden qualities of admirable literature

explanation: In this passage, the writer believes that half-educated persons cannot appreciate the hidden qualities of any admirable literature.

question: The word 'narcotic' in the passage means
explanation: Narcotic means a drug or intoxicant. Intoxicant means a narcotic/addictive substance.

question: The phrase 'the higher faculties' in the passage means

options.C: (c) mental powers of a high order for understanding great literature

explanation: 'Higher faculties' means option (c): mental powers of a high order for understanding great literature.

question: The word 'eventually' in the passage means
explanation: 'Eventually' means 'at last' (in the end/ultimately).

question: The word 'unemployed' in the passage means
explanation: According to the passage, 'unemployed' here means 'unused' – that which has not been used.

question: Which of the following words is 'plural'?
explanation: 'Mice' is a plural word because it considers the whole community of 'Rats', while Dog, Fox and Goat are singular words.

question: Which of the following words is regarded as singular?
explanation: When we use 's' after a noun, it becomes plural, as in Dogs, Cats and Cars, but 'Mathematics' is always a complete singular word. Each subject name is always singular.

question: Which of the following sentences is in active voice?

options.A: (a) He was asked to keep quiet.
options.B: (b) A house was bought for fifty lacs.
options.C: (c) I requested him to come in time.

explanation: A verb is said to be in the active voice when its subject acts or when the emphasis is on the doer, the person or thing denoted by the subject. The Active voice is so called because the subject is acting. Here subject 'I' is acting. Example: 'I' in the sentence performing the action and 'him' is a direct object.

question: Which of the following sentences is in passive voice?

options.B: (b) I kept quiet throughout the meeting.
options.C: (c) He was assisted by a group of students.
options.D: (d) They came late and lost the march.

explanation: A verb is in the passive voice when the subject is being acted upon or when the emphasis is on the subject or the work done. The passive voice is so called because the person or thing denoted by the subject receives some action. Here the subject 'He' is being acted upon.

question: 'That is the man the police was seeking.' In the above sentence, what is the status of the two 'the's?
explanation: 'A', 'An' and 'The' are articles. 'A' is used before the words which start with consonant (a, e, i, o, u) and 'An' is used before words which start with vowel. 'The' is used for definite things, so it is called the definite article.

question: 'These students are most disobedient.' In the above sentence, point out the gender of the word 'students.'
explanation: Common Gender – A type of gender that deals with the whole community including male, female and transgender. 'These students' here means male, female and transgender together. These students – themselves point out the community.

question: In the sentence 'He was going along the road', point out the tense.
explanation: Past Continuous – Rule of sentence: Subject + was/were + V-ing + Object. 'He was going along the road' is in the past continuous tense.

question: What is the main purpose of poetry recitation in a language classroom?

options.A: (a) To know the historical background of the poem
options.B: (b) To enjoy and appreciate the poem
options.C: (c) To give their opinions about the poem
options.D: (d) To become aware of the poet and his/her work

explanation: For the students of Class I to V, the teacher wishes only for his students to enjoy and appreciate the poem.

question: Fill in the blanks with the correct article: Yesterday I saw ………… European riding on ………… elephant.
explanation: Yesterday I saw 'a' European riding on 'an' elephant. E is a vowel but the article used is 'a' because the pronunciation of European starts with 'Eu' which sounds like the consonant 'Y'. But 'Elephant' uses 'An' by the rule of the article (starts with vowel sound).

question: Choose the correct sentence:
explanation: Please describe the story. Here 'Describe' contains no preposition. So 'describe about', 'describe to' etc. are wrong.

question: Fill in the blank with correct preposition: A prisoner was accused ………… murder.
explanation: In the sentence, the most suitable preposition is 'of'. 'Accused of' means to be charged with.

question: The antonym of 'innocent' is
explanation: The correct antonym of 'Innocent' is 'Guilty'. Innocent = बेकसूर, Guilty = अपराधी, Clever = चालाक, Active = सक्रिय, Ignorant = अज्ञानी.

question: The synonym of 'significant' is
explanation: The correct synonym of 'Significant' is 'Important'. Significant = महत्वपूर्ण – Important. Prominent = विशिष्ट, मुख्य; Efficient = कुशल, सक्षम; Magnificent = भव्य, शानदार.

question: Which one of the following words can be made plural by adding a suffix 'en'?
explanation: Suffix means letters which are used after words. Prefix means letters which are used before words. Ox + en = Oxen; Box + es = Boxes; Tax + es = Taxes; Max + es = Maxes.

question: Choose the correct pronoun to complete the following sentence: This dress is ……… and that one is mine.
explanation: This dress is yours and that one is mine. 'Yours' is a possessive pronoun and it correctly fills the blank.

question: Change the following sentence into passive voice: Have the girls asked this question?

options.A: (a) This question have asked by girls.
options.B: (b) Has this question been asked by the girls?
options.C: (c) Has this question asked by the girls?
options.D: (d) Have this question asked by the girls?

explanation: By the Rule of Active/Passive voice: Helping verb of Present Perfect Tense (has, have) changes into Present Perfect Passive (has been, have been). Therefore: Has this question been asked by the girls?

question: Which of the following is a material noun?
explanation: Material Noun – A kind of noun that refers to a material or substance from which things are made, such as silver, gold, iron, cotton, diamond and plastic. 'Gold' is a material noun.

question: Which of the following words is an abstract noun?
explanation: Abstract noun – A kind of noun that refers to an idea or a general quality, not to a physical object. Connection (संबंध, लगाव, जुड़ाव) is an abstract noun.

question: Which kind of noun is 'adversity'?
explanation: Adversity (कठिनाई, विपत्ति) is an abstract noun because it refers to an idea or quality rather than a physical thing.

question: Which part of speech is the word 'hard' in the sentence 'He is working hard'?
explanation: Hard (कठिन, मेहनतपूर्वक) is an adverb here. Adverb – A word that can be used before a noun, verb, a place, phrase or another verb for more information about time, manner, cause or degree.

question: Which of the following sentences has a 'conjunction'?

options.C: (c) She is poor but she is by nature hospitable.

explanation: 'But' is used in sentence (c) as a conjunction. Conjunction – A word that is used for joining other words, phrases or sentences.

question: Point out the sentence which is in present perfect tense.

options.A: (a) I shall go to Jaunpur tomorrow.

explanation: Rule of Present Perfect Tense: Subject + have/has + V₃ form + Object. I + have + finished + my work.

question: Which of the following is a misspelt word?
explanation: The correct spelling is 'Derogatory' not 'Derogatary'. Derogatory = अपमानजनक; Desiccation = निर्जलीकरण प्रक्रिया; Descendant = वंशज, संतान; Dermatitis = त्वचा की सूजन.

question: Which of the following sentences has a transitive verb?

options.A: (a) She walks in the morning daily.
options.D: (d) I killed a snake last night.

explanation: Transitive verb – A transitive verb is a verb that requires one or more objects. 'A snake' is the object in this sentence.

question: Which of the following sentences is negative?

options.A: (a) I come from a rich family.
options.B: (b) You can do all this in no time.
options.C: (c) He does not listen to me.

explanation: Negative sentence – If 'Not'/'Never' comes in a sentence, then the sentence is a negative sentence. 'He does not listen to me' is a negative sentence.

question: Which of the following sentences is exclamatory?

options.A: (a) What a piece of work is man.
options.B: (b) What do you know about ancient India.

explanation: Exclamatory sentence – This type of sentence deals with exclamation, a statement that conveys excitement or emotion abruptly. 'What a piece of work is man' is an exclamatory sentence.

question: Point out the complex sentence.

options.D: (d) One who does not live in one's country is a wretched person.

explanation: Complex sentence – This type of sentence has two or more sentences combined one to another. It also has two or more transitive verbs. 'One who does not live in one's country is a wretched person' is a complex sentence.

question: Which kind of noun is 'Gangapur'?
explanation: Gangapur is a name of a place. So it is a proper noun. Proper Noun – A name used for an individual person, place or organisation. Spelled with an initial capital letter. Example: Ram, Ganga, Lucknow etc.

question: Which part of speech is the word 'brave'?
explanation: Brave = बहादुर. Adjective – Adjectives are one of the English parts of speech. They tell you more about a noun.

question: Which of the following alternatives is the correct passive voice from the following sentence? 'Do it'
explanation: The given Active voice sentence is an Imperative sentence. Rule to change into Passive voice: A.V. = V 1st + object; P.V. = Let + object + be + V 3rd. Correct sentence: Let it be done.

question: Which of the following words is plural?
explanation: Criteria is a plural noun; its singular form is 'criterion'. Other words are Singular words.

question: Which of the following nouns is in plural?
explanation: Among the given words, 'mice' is a plural noun; its singular form is 'mouse'. Other words are singular words.

question: He was an orphan and lived with his uncle. In the above sentence identify the gender of the word 'orphan'.
explanation: The given word 'orphan' is a Common gender word. Common Gender – A noun that is used for both male (masculine) and female (feminine). It is called Common gender (Ubhayaling).

question: Which of the following words is not a co-ordinating conjunction?
explanation: The conjunction that forms a co-ordinate clause is called a co-ordinating conjunction. Among the given words, 'if' is a Sub-ordinating conjunction. Other words are co-ordinate conjunctions.

question: Fill in the blanks with appropriate prepositions: His thirst ………… knowledge left him no leisure ………… anything else.
explanation: The appropriate preposition 'for, for' will fill the blanks, because it is used in the sense of 'ke liye' (for). Correct sentence: His thirst for knowledge left him no leisure for anything else.

question: The word 'fast' in the sentence 'He is a fast writer' is
explanation: In the given sentence, 'fast' is describing the noun (writer). So it is an Adjective. Adjective – An adjective is a word used to qualify a noun or a pronoun.

question: Which of the following is not a simple sentence?

options.A: (a) She does not know good manners
options.C: (c) He is a man of great knowledge
options.D: (d) I have a very costly book in my house

explanation: Option (b) is an example of a complex sentence. All other options are simple sentences. Simple sentence – A simple sentence is one that has only one Finite verb.

question: The sentence 'He will be playing the piano in the concert day after tomorrow' is in:
explanation: The structure of the given sentence: Sub + will/shall be + V + ing + object + other words. This is Future continuous tense or Future imperfect tense, so option (b) is correct.

question: In which of the following sentences is the subject of verb a feminine gender noun?

options.A: (a) All the male members have arrived
options.B: (b) All the young men have arrived
options.C: (c) All the old men have arrived
options.D: (d) All the female members have arrived

explanation: Feminine gender – when the noun denotes a female (Female), it is called feminine gender word. Correct sentence: All the female members have arrived. Other options are wrong because they contain masculine gender words.

question: The method of teaching in which the teacher tries to establish a link between the foreign language word and the object without the interference of the learner's mother tongue is called:
explanation: Direct Method – A system of teaching a foreign language using only that language and without emphasis on the study of grammar. So the correct option is the direct method.

question: If you are testing your student's comprehension of written English, you are testing their understanding of what they have just
explanation: If you are testing students' comprehension of written English, you are testing their understanding of reading because this is the only way to measure language acquisition, communication, comprehension and sharing of information.

question: Find out the correct sentence/sentences:
explanation: Verbs of 'thinking' as: 'think, suppose, believe, agree, remember, understand' etc. are not normally used in the continuous form on account of their meaning. So option (a) is incorrect. Correct sentence: I don't believe him.

question: Which of the following alternatives is grammatically correct?
explanation: 'These' is used before Plural form of countable noun, so the correct sentence is: All these men are gentle. All other sentences are grammatically incorrect.

question: Fill in the blank with appropriate preposition: He came to me …………… midnight.
explanation: Preposition 'at' will be used in the blank. 'At' is used for short time and 'in' is used for long time. Correct sentence: He came to me at midnight.

question: Which of the following sentence does not have an adjective clause?

options.A: (a) I love the man who is truthful
options.B: (b) I met him where he lived
options.C: (c) I met him in Prayagraj which is a holy city.
options.D: (d) The man who is truthful is loved by all.

explanation: Adjective Clause – A subordinate clause begins with a relative pronoun or relative adverb that always depends on an antecedent. In option (b) 'where' is used as a noun clause, while in other options Adjective clause is used.

question: Fill in the blank with correct preposition: He is junior …………… me.
explanation: Preposition 'to' will be used in the blank. Because some words – superior, junior, inferior etc. always use 'to' preposition in the sense of comparison. Correct sentence: He is junior to me.

question: Fill in the blank with correct conjunction: Either he is mad ………… he feigns madness.
explanation: The correct word 'or' will be used in the blank. Because the correct pair of 'Either' is 'or'. Note: Either – or; Neither – nor; Both – and; Though – yet; Whether – or. Correct sentence: Either he is mad or he feigns madness.

question: Which of the following sentence/sentences is/are correct? (A) There is few hope of his recovery. (B) He showed few concern for his nephew. (C) He showed little mercy to the vanquished.
explanation: 'Few' is used before countable nouns (negative sense) while 'little' is used before uncountable nouns (negative sense) singular form. Hope, Concern are uncountable nouns. So 'few' cannot be used before them. Correct sentence: He showed little mercy to the vanquished.

question: Point out the sentence which is in past perfect tense.

options.A: (a) I had written my letter before he arrived.
options.B: (b) We were listening to the radio all evening.

explanation: Past perfect tense structure: Sub + had + V 3rd + object + other word. Correct sentence: I had written my letter before he arrived.

question: Fill in the blanks with the correct article: Neil Armstrong was …………… first man to walk on ………… moon.
explanation: The ordinal adjective 'first' uses the article 'the' before it, and 'The' is used before names of unique things like sun, moon, earth, stars etc. Correct sentence: Neil Armstrong was the first man to walk on the moon.

question: Fill in the blank in the following sentence: The teacher has been teaching for ………… hour.
explanation: After the blank, the word 'hour' starts with a vowel sound pronunciation, so vowel article 'an' will be used. Correct sentence: The teacher has been teaching for an hour.

question: Add the right suffix to pluralize the word 'Ox'
explanation: By adding suffix 'en' to 'Ox', its plural is made. Correct word: Oxen.

question: Change the word 'grow' into a noun by adding one of the suffixes given below.
explanation: The given word 'grow' is a verb, so by adding suffix 'th' to it we make a noun. Correct word: growth.

question: The word 'strength' is a
explanation: Strength – मजबूती is an Abstract noun word. Abstract Noun (भाववाचक संज्ञा) – refers to an idea or quality; it tells the name of quality, action or state of any subject/thing. Examples: Quality (गुण) – Goodness, bravery etc.; Action (क्रिया) – Laughter, theft, movement etc.

question: One who does not take revenge is:
explanation: According to the passage's first line, one who does not take revenge means to forgive, which is really a sign of strength.

question: What is the meaning of the word 'untrodden'?
explanation: Untrodden = संक्षिप्त; Explored = बृहद बनाना, व्याख्या करना; Hidden = छिपा हुआ; Explicit = स्पष्ट; Unexplored = संक्षिप्त. The correct meaning of 'Untrodden' is 'Unexplored' (not trodden upon, unexplored).

question: Identify the correct figure of speech used in the second stanza.
explanation: In the second Stanza, 'as' is used. So it is a Simile. Simile – A figure of speech that directly compares two different things with the words 'as' or 'like'. Example: 'Fair as star when only one is shining in the sky.'

question: Choose an appropriate conjunction for the following sentence: Strike the iron _________ it is hot.
explanation: The appropriate conjunction 'while' will be used in the blank because according to the meaning of the sentence, other conjunctions are not suitable. Correct sentence: 'Strike the iron while it is hot.' (Strike the iron when it is hot / लोहे पर प्रहार करो जब यह गर्म हो).

question: Identify the correct passive voice for the sentence: 'Please sit down'

options.C: (c) You are requested to sit down

explanation: The given sentence 'Please sit down' is an Imperative sentence. Its passive voice is 'You are requested to sit down'. Rule for passive voice of Imperative sentence starting with please/kindly: You are requested to + MV + Object.

question: The use of technology to enhance the learning process is called ………… in education.
explanation: The use of technology to enhance the learning process is called ICT in education. Information and Communication Technology, usually abbreviated as ICT, refers to the mode of education that uses innovative technology to support and enhance learning. ICT tools for teaching and learning cover everything from digital infrastructures such as printers, computers, laptops, tablets, to software tools such as Google Meet, Google Spreadsheets, etc.

question: The sentence 'John will be leaving for England tomorrow' is in:
explanation: The given sentence 'John will be leaving for England tomorrow' is in future continuous tense. Structure of future continuous tense: Sub + will be/shall be + v + ing form + object.

question: Which of the following is a complex sentence?

options.A: (a) She lives in a locality of her liking.
options.B: (b) Ravi did not like that bearded man.
options.D: (d) Ramesh is a man of robust body.

explanation: In option (c), 'He reads what he likes' is a complex sentence. A complex sentence is formed by adding one or more subordinate (dependent) clauses to the main (independent) clause using conjunctions like what/where (relative pronouns). Example: He forgot to give the teacher his homework when he came late to class.

question: The teachers in English Medium Schools usually teach their students in:
explanation: The teachers in English Medium Schools usually teach their students in the structural method. The structural approach is a language approach that gives priority to pupils' activity, spoken language, multiskill approach, situational teaching, etc. The modern trend of having a systematic and scientific attitude towards language teaching has evolved the Structural Approach to language teaching.

question: Find out the subject, in the following sentence: 'The beautiful rainbow soon faded away'
explanation: In the given sentence 'The beautiful rainbow soon faded away', the subject is 'the beautiful rainbow'. A subject is a noun or pronoun. Rainbow (इन्द्रधनुष) is a noun and beautiful is an adjective which adds more information about the noun. Hence, option (d) is correct.

question: Supply the correct article in the following sentence: Raghu is ……………… one-eyed man.
explanation: In the blank space, article 'a' will be used because the word 'one-eyed' starts with 'o' which is a consonant sound (Constant), so 'a' article is used before it. Before Vowels (a, e, i, o, u) 'an' article is used. Correct sentence: Raghu is a one-eyed man.

question: Which of the following sentence is in Passive Voice?

options.C: (c) A letter was written by him yesterday

explanation: 'A letter was written by him yesterday' is in passive voice. Structure of passive voice: Object + was/were + V3rd form + by + subject. Note: If the given sentence is in active voice and in past indefinite tense, when changing to passive, past continuous (was/were) changes.

question: Fill in the blank with correct preposition: Neither he is sorry ………… his father.
explanation: 'Nor' will be used in the blank because 'nor' is used as a conjunction after 'Neither'. Note: Important conjunction pairs: Either – or; Neither – nor; Both – and; Though – yet; Whether – or. Correct sentence: Neither he is sorry nor his father.

question: Add the right suffix to pluralise the word 'Dictionary':
explanation: The word 'Dictionary' uses the right suffix 'ies'. Dictionary + ies = Dictionaries. Other options are not suitable. Hence option (c) is correct answer.

question: Fill in the blank with appropriate preposition in the following sentence: I must congratulate you ………… your exam results.
explanation: Preposition 'on' will be used in the blank because 'on' is used after 'congratulate' – 'Congratulate on'. Note: 'at' is used with short time references and 'in' is used with long time references. Correct sentence: I must congratulate you on your exam results.

question: Fill in the blank with suitable article in the following sentence: Yesterday ………… European called at my office.
explanation: Suitable article 'a' will be used in the blank because the word 'European' starts with 'y' sound which is a consonant sound, so 'a' article is used. Correct sentence: Yesterday a European called at my office.

question: Fill in the blanks with suitable articles in the following sentence: ………… Ganga is ………… sacred river.
explanation: 'The' and 'a' articles will be used in the blanks. Ganga is a particular river so 'The' is used before it. Correct sentence: The Ganga is a sacred river.

question: Fill in the blank with correct conjunction: Walk quickly ………… you will not over-take him.
explanation: 'Else' will be used in the blank because as a conjunction, 'else' is more suitable than other options. Correct sentence: Walk quickly else you will not over-take him.

question: Fill in the blanks with the correct article: ………… Rajdhani express is considered as one of ………… fastest train in India.
explanation: 'The, the' will be used in the blanks because here the first sentence is about train and the second sentence uses superlative degree. Before both 'The' article is used. Correct sentence: The Rajdhani express is considered as one of the fastest train in India.

question: Which prefix can be used before all the following words: proper, polite, mortal, movable
explanation: The correct prefix 'Im-' can be used before all the given words (Proper, Polite, Mortal, Movable). Meanings: Improper – अनुचित, Impolite – असभ्य, Immortal – अमर, Immovable – अचल.

question: Plural of maximum is …………
explanation: The plural of 'maximum' is 'maxima', meaning the most/highest. Hence option (a) is correct.

question: Add the right suffix to pluralize the word 'Memento'
explanation: The word 'Memento' uses the right suffix '_es', giving 'mementoes' (plural noun). Meaning of Memento – Something that you keep to remind you of somebody/something (स्मृति-चिन्ह).

question: Identify the gender of the word 'czarina'
explanation: The word 'czarina' is the feminine gender. Czarina = रूस की साम्राज्ञी (female ruler of Russia). Feminine gender – A gender that denotes a female, it is called feminine gender. Examples: Girl, women, mother, wife etc.

question: Fill in the blank with the appropriate preposition: The coolie carried a suitcase ………… his head.
explanation: Appropriate preposition 'on' will be used in the blank because 'on' is used to express the feeling of 'place-touch'. Correct sentence: The coolie carried a suitcase on his head.

question: Which of the following word is plural?
explanation: Among the given options, 'Alumni' is plural. Its singular form is 'Alumnus'. Meanings: Alumni – पूर्व छात्र; Oasis – मरुस्थल के बीच हरित भूमि; Radius – त्रिज्या; Stadium – क्रीड़ा स्थल.

question: What is the meaning of the word 'flake'? [From poem: 'The only other sound's the sweep / Of easy wind and downy flake.']
explanation: In the given poem, the meaning of the word 'flake' is 'A small thin piece' (a small, thin piece, here referring to a snowflake). Drops of rain = वर्षा की बूँदें.

question: 'deep' and 'keep' are …………
explanation: 'Deep' and 'keep' are Rhyming words. Rhyming words – Two or more words that have the same or similar ending sound.

question: The Ganga is a symbol of:
explanation: The Ganga is a symbol of 'India's age long culture and civilization'. The Ganga is a symbol of faith, hope, culture and sanity, as well as a source of livelihood for millions since time immemorial.

question: What are the things loved by the author mentioned in the given passage?

options.A: (a) The snow-covered peaks and the deep valleys of the Himalayas
options.D: (d) The snowy peaks and the deep valleys of the Himalayas as well as the rich and vast plains below.

explanation: In the given passage, the author mentioned the snowy peaks and the deep valleys of the Himalayas as well as the rich and vast plains below.

question: The meaning of the idiom 'to put out' is
explanation: The meaning of the idiom 'to put out' is 'To extinguish' (बुझाना).

question: The sentence: 'This is the house in which I was born', is
explanation: The given sentence is a complex sentence because a complex sentence has at least two clauses – one independent and one dependent (subordinate). 'This is the house' is the independent clause and 'in which I was born' is the dependent clause.

question: Fill in the blank: 'The objection was met out ______ the lawyer.'
explanation: The sentence is in Passive voice. 'By' is used in Passive voice constructions. A.V. = Sub + was + VI + object; P.V. = object + was + VIII + by + sub.

question: Fill in the blank: '______ of playing, the children went home'.
explanation: 'Having tired' is correct because after one action is completed, another begins. Perfect Participle (having + Verb 3) is used. Correct Sentence – Having tired of playing, the children went home.

question: Join the following sentence to make a simple sentence: 'The coffee isn't too strong. It won't keep awake.'

options.A: The coffee isn't too strong so it won't keep us awake.
options.B: The coffee isn't too strong and will not keep us awake.
options.C: The coffee isn't too strong to keep us awake.
options.D: The coffee isn't too strong so as to keep us awake.

explanation: Simple sentence is formed by combining multiple clauses. The correct answer is 'The coffee isn't too strong to keep us awake.'

question: What kind of adjectives are used in the following sentence? 'A live ass is better than a dead lion.'
explanation: The sentence 'A live ass is better than a dead lion' uses Quality adjectives – Live, better, and dead are qualitative adjectives describing the qualities of Ass and Lion.

question: Which of the following words has been misspelt?
explanation: Option (a) 'governence' is misspelt. The correct spelling is 'governance' (with 'a' instead of 'e').

question: Pick out the correct synonym of the word: attenuate
explanation: The correct synonym of 'Attenuate' is 'make thin' (कमजोर करना).

question: Pick the sentence with the correct conjunction:

options.A: No sooner had we reached the station when the train left.
options.B: No sooner had we reached the station than the train left.
options.C: No sooner had we reached the station but the train left.
options.D: No sooner had we reached the station and the train left.

explanation: 'No sooner' is used with 'than'. The correct pattern is: No sooner + had/did + than. Also 'No sooner' requires inversion.

question: Pick out the suitable antonym of the given word: judicious
explanation: The suitable antonym of 'Judicious' (विवेकपूर्ण) is 'Imprudent' (अविवेकी).

question: What does 'encore' mean? (from passage)
explanation: In the given passage, 'Encore' (दोहराना) means 'once more' (फिर से).

question: What did Kim do in the end? (from passage)
explanation: From the passage's second paragraph, when children shouted 'once more', Kim gave them a bright smile and vanished from the scene. Answer: Smiled and disappeared.

question: What does 'Century' denote? (from passage)
explanation: A period of hundred years (सौ साल की अवधि) is called 'Century'.

question: What is the magician's name? (from passage)
explanation: From the passage, the magician's name is 'Kim Rathod'.

question: What is the meaning of 'captivated'? (from passage)
explanation: The meaning of 'Captivated' is 'मोहित होना' (Arrested / आकर्षित होना).

question: What is the meaning of 'spontaneous'?
explanation: The meaning of 'Spontaneous' is 'स्वतः या स्वाभाविक' which is 'Happening suddenly' (option C).

question: What is a 'Wand'?
explanation: The literal meaning of 'Wand' is 'छड़ी' (Stick).

question: What is the meaning of 'hopping'?
explanation: The meaning of 'Hopping' is 'Jumping' (फुदकना होना).

question: Complete the following sentence with correct conjunction: '______ he had not paid his bill, his electricity was cut off.'
explanation: 'As' is used as conjunction because it connects words, phrases and clauses. As he had not paid his bill, his electricity was cut off.

question: Choose the correct figure of speech in the following sentence: 'Death lays his icy hand on Kings.'
explanation: The figure of speech in 'Death lays his icy hand on Kings' is Personification – inanimate things and emotions are portrayed as persons.

question: Choose the proper sequence to complete: 'The guide said that / P. nowhere in the world / Q. a fairer building / R. you will find / S. than the Taj Mahal.'
explanation: The proper sequence is PRQS: 'The guide said that nowhere in the world you will find a fairer building than the Taj Mahal.'

question: Give the meaning of the following proverb: 'A bird in hand is worth two in a bush.'

options.A: It is better to be satisfied with what you have than to try to get what is not yours.
options.B: To be dissatisfied with what you have.
options.C: To catch two birds with your hands.

explanation: The proverb 'A bird in hand is worth two in a bush' means 'It is better to be satisfied with what you have than to try to get what is not yours.'

question: Choose the correct option to complete the sentence: 'I ______ him since we met a year ago.'
explanation: 'Haven't seen' is used because Conjunction 'since' correlates Past to Present – 'Since we met a year ago, I haven't seen him.'

question: Fill in the blank with suitable Pronoun: 'My son and my daughter are very fond of ______.'
explanation: 'Each other' is used for two persons. My son and my daughter are very fond of each other.

question: Which of the following is an example of Metaphor?

options.A: The bitter-sweet memories of childhood are a source of great pleasure.
options.B: The face of the child is as soft as the petals of a rose.
options.D: My heart leaps up when I behold a rainbow in the sky.

explanation: 'He is a big donkey' is an example of Metaphor as a person is described in the form of 'Big donkey'.

question: Choose the correct option: 'Study of population is known as'
explanation: 'Study of population' is called 'Demography'. Astronomy = Study of the universe; Ecology = Study of environment; Genealogy = Study of the ancestors' history.

question: Transform the following into a Direct sentence: 'I asked Sheila if I might know her father.'

options.A: I asked Sheila, 'Can I know your father?'
options.B: I told Sheila, 'May I know your father?'
options.C: I said to Sheila, 'May I know your father?'
options.D: I questioned Sheila, 'Will I know your father?'

explanation: Converting Indirect to Direct: 'Asked' changes to 'said to'; conjunction 'if' is removed, inverted comma is used; 'Might' changes to 'May' and structure becomes Interrogative.

question: The idiom 'To ring one's own bell' means
explanation: 'To ring one's own bell' means 'to indulge in self praise' (अपना ढिंढोरा खुद पिटाना).
